items.title: calibration collapse under ai acceleration
items.summary: LLM assistance attacks the feedback loop that used to turn visible failure into evaluative skill.

items.title: commercial deployment as governance bypass
items.summary: AI governance loses force when contested behavior is installed as ordinary infrastructure before anyone is asked to consent.

items.title: theory death at production speed
items.summary: AI coding makes code production cheap enough that clean artifacts can outrun the shared theory needed to change them.

items.title: signal-property decoupling
items.summary: AI makes old signals cheap enough that communities and evaluators can no longer trust the properties they used to imply.

items.title: behavioral assertions over structural gaps
items.summary: Security promises fail predictably when the structure underneath them still lets the forbidden move happen.

items.title: behavioral portability and structural lock-in
items.summary: GitHub exit looks easy at the repository layer and hard everywhere software work became platform-issued behavior.

items.title: trust primitives stop at admission
items.summary: Web and package security both keep proving admission while leaving the admitted session under-modeled.

items.title: structural guarantees as compressed optionality
items.summary: Structural guarantees buy certainty by deleting the option to recover the old degrees of freedom later.

items.title: execution context integrity
items.summary: Supply-chain security keeps proving what was fetched while attackers move the point where fetched code becomes running code.

items.title: github institutional memory loss
items.summary: Git is decentralized, but GitHub made open source memory behaviorally centralized.

items.title: ebpf dpi bypass as timing desynchronization
items.summary: A timing-based DPI bypass is not a failure of behavioral inspection so much as a structural commitment problem.

items.title: nan-boxing inversion
items.summary: NaN-boxing is the odd case where IEEE 754 behavior enables a structural runtime type encoding.

items.title: structural encoding in 90s simulation games
items.summary: 1990s simulation games often got realism by encoding constraints into structure because the CPU budget left no room for richer behavior.

items.title: behavioral commitments without enforcement ceilings
items.summary: AI governance keeps describing intended conduct while leaving revision, verification, and exit in behavior rather than structure.

items.title: confabulation as structural property
items.summary: LLM confabulation is an architectural consequence of statistical completion without a native ignorance state.

items.title: ai traffic observability asymmetry
items.summary: AI traffic is not one measurement problem. Some traffic is missing because of architecture, and some is hidden by behavior.

items.title: on structural content integrity
items.summary: Most static site generators discover content violations at the wrong time. A case for making them type errors instead.
